[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property - Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property - Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉|12=draft}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=draft}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永|12=draft}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=draft}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|12=draft}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=draft}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動|12=draft}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助|12=draft}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=draft}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館|12=draft}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |location = {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}} |Shrine rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} (August) }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|ja|安曇野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉|12=draft}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=draft}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei 8|ja|応永|12=draft}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=draft}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|ja|文禄|12=draft}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=draft}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō Uprising|ja|貞享騒動|12=draft}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|ja|多田加助|12=draft}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=draft}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|ja|貞享義民記念館|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|お船祭り|" to "|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|"
1 revision imported: Bot: import full history from ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市) up to rev 75471621
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 熊野神社 |画像 = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[安曇野市]]三郷明盛3319番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 伊弉冊神、速玉男命、泉津事解男命、素戔嗚尊 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[お船祭り]]（毎年8月） |神事 = }} '''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[安曇野市]]にある神社。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。
== 祭神 == 祭神は以下の4柱<ref>長野県町村誌 1876年</ref>。 *伊弉冊神 *速玉男命 *泉津事解男命 *素戔嗚尊
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[白雉]]元年（650年）に[[熊野権現]]を勧請し、[[応永]]8年（1401年）に再度、熊野三社を勧請した。当社の南側にあった神宮寺は、応永18年（1411年）熊野から来た僧が創建し、十一面観世音菩薩の立像を安置したことによる。[[文禄]]時代の検地では社領5石が安堵されているが、これは[[安曇郡]]内では[[穂高神社]]、[[住吉神社 (安曇野市)|住吉神社]]に次ぐ処遇であった。[[貞享]]3年（1686年）の[[貞享騒動]]では[[多田加助]]ら主導者が、当社の境内で一揆の誓詞を交わした。明治35年（1902年）、村社から郷社となり、明治40年（1907年）下中萱の八坂社を合祀、[[大正]]12年（1923年）には伏見稲荷を勧請した。
毎年8月には[[お船祭り]]が行われ、壮大な山車（お船）が曳航される<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り]安曇野市</ref>。
== 文化財 == *熊野神社本殿（安曇野市有形文化財） *旧八坂社本殿（安曇野市有形文化財） *絵馬（安曇野市有形民俗文化財） **花鳥の図、山姥と金太郎、大江山酒天童子、素盞嗚尊八岐大蛇、武者絵、福禄寿、鎌倉大評定、巴御前 *お船祭り（安曇野市無形民俗文化財）
== 所在地 == * 長野県安曇野市三郷明盛3319番地
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == *『三郷村誌 民俗編』 2004年
== 周辺 == *[[貞享義民記念館]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くまのしんしや あつみの}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[Category:安曇野市の歴史]] [[Category:安曇野市の建築物]] [[Category:熊野神社]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 熊野神社 |画像 = [[File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[安曇野市]]三郷明盛3319番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|16|24.15|N|137|53|44.23|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 伊弉冊神、速玉男命、泉津事解男命、素戔嗚尊 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[お船祭り]]（毎年8月） |神事 = }} '''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[安曇野市]]にある神社。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。
== 祭神 == 祭神は以下の4柱<ref>長野県町村誌 1876年</ref>。 *伊弉冊神 *速玉男命 *泉津事解男命 *素戔嗚尊
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[白雉]]元年（650年）に[[熊野権現]]を勧請し、[[応永]]8年（1401年）に再度、熊野三社を勧請した。当社の南側にあった神宮寺は、応永18年（1411年）熊野から来た僧が創建し、十一面観世音菩薩の立像を安置したことによる。[[文禄]]時代の検地では社領5石が安堵されているが、これは[[安曇郡]]内では[[穂高神社]]、[[住吉神社 (安曇野市)|住吉神社]]に次ぐ処遇であった。[[貞享]]3年（1686年）の[[貞享騒動]]では[[多田加助]]ら主導者が、当社の境内で一揆の誓詞を交わした。明治35年（1902年）、村社から郷社となり、明治40年（1907年）下中萱の八坂社を合祀、[[大正]]12年（1923年）には伏見稲荷を勧請した。
毎年8月には[[お船祭り]]が行われ、壮大な山車（お船）が曳航される<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り]安曇野市</ref>。
== 文化財 == *熊野神社本殿（安曇野市有形文化財） *旧八坂社本殿（安曇野市有形文化財） *絵馬（安曇野市有形民俗文化財） **花鳥の図、山姥と金太郎、大江山酒天童子、素盞嗚尊八岐大蛇、武者絵、福禄寿、鎌倉大評定、巴御前 *お船祭り（安曇野市無形民俗文化財）
== 所在地 == * 長野県安曇野市三郷明盛3319番地
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == *『三郷村誌 民俗編』 2004年
== 周辺 == *[[貞享義民記念館]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85882245]] Wikidata name: Kumano-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ([[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|qid=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>

'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85882245]] Wikidata name: Kumano-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ([[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85882245]] Wikidata name: Kumano-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ([[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85882245]] Wikidata name: Kumano-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ([[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85882245]] Wikidata name: Kumano-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ([[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85882245]] Wikidata name: Kumano-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ([[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Azumino|Azumino]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei 8]] 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku]] era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the [[:en:Jōkyō uprising|Jōkyō Uprising]] in 1686 when [[:en:Tada Kasuke|Tada Kasuke]] and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * [[:en:Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall]]
