<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi (era)|en|Hakuchi (era)|WD=Q540118|lt=Hakuchi}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei 8}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō uprising|en|Jōkyō uprising|WD=Q6322226|lt=Jōkyō Uprising}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|en|Tada Kasuke|WD=Q7674262|lt=Tada Kasuke}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|en|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|WD=Q6322223|lt=Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi (era)|en|Hakuchi (era)|WD=Q540118|lt=Hakuchi}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei 8}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō uprising|en|Jōkyō uprising|WD=Q6322226|lt=Jōkyō Uprising}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|en|Tada Kasuke|WD=Q7674262|lt=Tada Kasuke}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|en|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|WD=Q6322223|lt=Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall}}

== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|en|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|WD=Q6322223|lt=Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|1=Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kumano shrine in Azumino, where Tada Kasuke and his followers gathered.JPG |location = {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino|1=Azumino}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Misato Akari 3319 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.273375|N|137.895619|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Gōsha ({{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}) |Foundation = |Main building = |Other names = |festival = {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} (August) |location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667|1=Azumino}} }}
'''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino|1=Azumino}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:<ref>長野県町村誌, 1876</ref> * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja|泉津事解男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was first established in {{ill|Hakuchi (era)|en|Hakuchi (era)|WD=Q540118|lt=Hakuchi|1=Hakuchi (era)}} 1 (650) when {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined. It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei 8|1=Ōei}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506333|1=1411}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō uprising|en|Jōkyō uprising|WD=Q6322226|lt=Jōkyō Uprising|1=Jōkyō uprising}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|en|Tada Kasuke|WD=Q7674262|lt=Tada Kasuke|1=Tada Kasuke}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|en|Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum|WD=Q6322223|lt=Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Hall|1=Jōkyō Gimin Memorial Museum}}

It was later rebuilt in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|qid=Q1193078|lt=Ōei 8|1=Ōei}} 8 (1401) to enshrine the three Kumano deities. A temple known as Jingūji was established on the southern grounds in {{ill|Ōei 18|ja|応永18|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei 18|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506333|1=1411}} (1411) by a monk from Kumano and housed a standing statue of the Eleven-Faced Kannon.
During the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|qid=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} era (1592–1596), the shrine received an allotment of five koku of shrine land, Ranking it among the most significant shrines in Azumi District, alongside {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|qid=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉神社 (安曇野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine played a pivotal role during the {{ill|Jōkyō uprising|en|Jōkyō uprising|qid=Q6322226|lt=Jōkyō Uprising|1=Jōkyō uprising}} in 1686 when {{ill|Tada Kasuke|en|Tada Kasuke|qid=Q7674262|lt=Tada Kasuke|1=Tada Kasuke}} and his followers swore oaths of rebellion on its grounds.
In 1902, the shrine was elevated to the Rank of Gōsha. In 1907, it merged with the Yasaka Shrine of Shimonakakaya, and in 1923, it enshrined {{ill|Inari|ja|伏見稲荷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伏見稲荷大社|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q714828}}.
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}, held in August, features elaborate floats known as "ships" (お船) that are paraded through the area.<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り], Azumino City</ref>
== Cultural Properties == The shrine and its associated properties have been designated as cultural assets by Azumino City: * '''Main Shrine Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Former Yasaka Shrine Main Building''' – City Tangible Cultural Property * '''Ema (Votive Paintings)''' – City Tangible Folk Cultural Property ** Examples include depictions of flora and fauna, Yamamba and Kintarō, Shuten-dōji at Ōeyama, Susanoo slaying the Yamata-no-Orochi, warrior portraits, and the story of Tomoe Gozen. * '''Ofune Matsuri''' – City Intangible Folk Cultural Property
== location == * A11ress: 3319 Misato Akari, Azumino City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan
== References == <references/>
